prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: J. G. Ballard is an under-discussed writer, often known for his post-apocalyptic novel "The Drowned World" or his controversial work "Crash." However, what many miss about Ballard is his profound insight into the psychological effects of modernity and technological progress. His writing, as seen in "The Atrocity Exhibition" and "High-Rise," dissects the alienation and disillusionment that can result from the breakneck pace of change, as well as the darker impulses that can surface when societal constructs break down. Ballard's unflinching yet clinical examination of human nature under duress is both chilling and thought-provoking.
